1_cabinet_type_selection.principle: Cabinet type based on location, equipment count, and environment

types.best_for: Small IDF closets, offices, retail back rooms, apartments
types.mounting: Wall-mounted on concrete/block wall with 4+ anchor bolts

types.best_for: Server rooms, MDF, data centers, comms rooms
types.mounting: Floor-standing with leveling feet or casters

types.best_for: Pole-mounted for CCTV, parking lots, remote sites, outdoor PoE switches
Behind TV, small equipment, patch panel only

2_rack_unit_sizing.principle: Calculate total U needed then add 30% spare capacity
2_rack_unit_sizing.example: 16-camera system: 1U patch panel + 1U cable manager + 1U PoE switch + 1.5U NVR + 2U UPS + 1U fan tray + 1U blank = 8.5U → select 12U cabinet (with 30% spare)
2_rack_unit_sizing.rule: Always round UP and add minimum 2U spare for future expansion

3_depth_selection.principle: Cabinet depth must exceed deepest equipment + cable bend radius

guide.fits: Patch panels, small switches, basic NVR
Most PoE switches, NVRs, small UPS
Full-depth switches, rack-mount UPS, servers up to 400mm
guide.best_for: Standard floor standing, larger wall mount

guide.fits: Standard servers, large UPS, deep switches
Full-depth servers, enterprise UPS, blade systems

3_depth_selection.cable_clearance: Allow minimum 100mm behind equipment for cable bending radius

4_width_selection.principle: Standard 19" (482.6mm) equipment width — cabinet external width varies

widths.542mm: Economy — fits 19" equipment with minimal side clearance
widths.600mm: Standard — fits 19" equipment with side cable routing
widths.800mm: Wide — fits 19" equipment with vertical cable management channels on both sides

4_width_selection.recommendation: 600mm for most installations. 800mm when vertical cable management is needed (>24 cables) or for dense server installations.

5_outdoor_cabinet_design.principle: Outdoor cabinets protect equipment from weather, dust, and vandalism
features: IP55 rated (dust protected + water jet protected)
Pole-mount bracket for street/parking lot installation
typical_use: Hikvision PoE switch + fiber media converter for remote camera cluster
5_outdoor_cabinet_design.sizing: 4U for 1 switch + patch panel. 6U for switch + NVR. 9U for full system with UPS.

6_cooling_and_ventilation.principle: Equipment generates heat — cooling prevents premature failure

rules.cooling: 1U thermostat fan unit (4 fans, temp-controlled) + perforated rear door

6_cooling_and_ventilation.thermostat_fan: 1U Fan Unit with Thermostat ($55-65): fans activate automatically when cabinet temp exceeds set threshold (typically 35°C). Essential for enclosed floor-standing cabinets.
6_cooling_and_ventilation.ambient_temp: Cabinet should be in air-conditioned room for loads >500W. Max ambient 40°C.

7_pdu_selection.principle: PDU provides power distribution to all rack equipment

options.use: Budget, wall mount cabinets, light loads

7_pdu_selection.plug_type: UK plug (Type G) — standard for Jordan
7_pdu_selection.recommendation: Use metal PDU for floor-standing cabinets (better grounding, surge protection). 1 PDU per side for redundancy in critical installations.

8_cable_management.principle: Proper cable management improves airflow, access, and appearance

components.horizontal_cable_manager: 1U cable organizer between every 2U of patch panels/switches ($5-6)
components.vertical_cable_management: Built into 800mm wide cabinets. Routes cables from patch panel to switch ports neatly.
components.brush_panel: Allows cables to pass through while blocking airflow leakage ($5)
components.blank_panel: Covers unused U space to maintain front-to-back airflow ($3-5)

8_cable_management.rule: Every patch panel should have a 1U cable manager directly below it. Fill all empty U spaces with blank panels.

9_expert_vs_schneider_cabinets.principle: Expert (EX) cabinets are budget-friendly local option vs Schneider international brand

comparison.strengths: Competitive local pricing, full range, outdoor options, accessories included
International brand, IP30 rated, RAL7035 finish

9_expert_vs_schneider_cabinets.note: Expert cabinets include fans, shelves, and PDU at listed price. Schneider cabinets are typically sold bare — accessories extra.

standards.scope: Data center infrastructure (for server room cabinets)
Fire protection for IT equipment rooms (US)
